author: @saifcathum3423
text: Cool. My stick welds never came out that good when I was starting out. Took some practice to strike an arc without sticking the electrode. Love electric bikes so much so that my car and motorcycle have been collecting dust for a while now.

author: @justinw1765
text:
Nice job.  I figure that metal is attractive because welding is relatively easy (once learned and you have equipment), fast, and convenient, but have you thought of trying a composites approach?  You can save a lot of weight, and when it comes to E vehicles, weight (and aerodynamics) is critical.  

   Nor do you need expensive, fancy carbon fiber to get there.  Some lighter weight wood as the core (probably best being Paulownia), some S glass fiberglass, some high quality epoxy, and/or some carbonized cellulose nanocrystals makes some pretty dang strong but lightweight composites. There are now some at least partially bio based epoxies out there.

  And bamboo reinforced with the above materials, makes awesome tubes.

author: @uncledodge9396
text: What motor controller, motor and throttle did you use, and where do you get these from?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: The motor I am using is a 500w 24v dc motor this was from amazon, the throttle was one we had floating about so I am unsure where this came from, and the motor controller was from ebay its a 500w 24v motor controller. I will go into more detail in part 2 though. Hope this helps. :)
